title: In southern Beirut: 'Dahiyeh is destroyed, but that changes nothing for us. We support Hezbollah.'
subhed: On the outskirts of the deserted Shiite neighborhoods in the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, militants and a few remaining residents watch helplessly as Israeli strikes destroy entire areas, day after day.

title: Hungarian PM Orban campaigns on fears of war and hatred of Ukraine
subhed: Threatened in the polls for the April parliamentary elections by Peter Magyar's party, nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban has portrayed his rival as an agent who serves Kyiv and the European Union.

title: Far right lays trap for Dati in Paris mayoral race
subhed: Rachida Dati, the conservative candidate for mayor of Paris, finds herself in an uncomfortable position as Eric Zemmour's party calls for an alliance in the second round.

title: PSG is struggling, but not yet out of the running
subhed: Paris Saint-Germain, which faces Chelsea on Wednesday in the round of 16 of the Champions League, has lost the dominance it displayed last season. Nevertheless, the current campaign is far from over, writes journalist Jérôme Latta.

title: 'The tendency to treat a local election as a national test may seem surprising'
subhed: With just 14 months to go before the 2027 presidential election, France's municipal elections are seen as a major political test by candidates seeking momentum, explains Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhed: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

title: Trump seeks way out of Iran war but struggles to define it
subhed: Speaking from Florida on Monday, the US president highlighted Washington's military successes but failed to offer a clear path out of the conflict, fueling the impression of ongoing improvisation.

title: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
subhed: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.
